title: Bounded factor-certificate scaling experiment — CONSISTENT with QA_PRIME_BOUNDED_CERTIFICATE_SCALING_CERT.v1 on tested endpoints
_fixture_purpose: PASS fixture: repo-native empirical observation tying the real bounded-certificate scaling experiment artifact to the new formal cert family. The narrow claim is empirical only: on tested endpoints 100, 250, 500, and 1000, the minimal passing bounded witness cap matched the largest prime <= sqrt(N).

observation.summary: The bounded-certificate scaling experiment passed on endpoints 100, 250, 500, and 1000. Observed minimal passing prime_max values were 7, 13, 19, and 31, exactly matching the largest prime <= sqrt(N) at each endpoint.

parent_cert.claim: On the tested endpoints, the observed minimal passing prime_max matches the largest prime <= sqrt(N)

evidence.description: Observed and predicted bounds agree on every tested endpoint

open_questions: Does the same scaling law persist on larger endpoints beyond 1000?
Can this empirical law be strengthened into a theorem without collapsing back to ordinary smallest-factor reasoning?

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)
